question.content: For the overhanging simply supported beam model, find both the maximum bending normal stress and the maximum shear stress. Answer format: sigma_max/tau_max, in this order, with no explanatory text or equals signs.

step_1.content: Set unknown support reactions as \(F_{R,A}\) at x=2 m and \(F_{R,B}\) at x=6 m. External loads are 10 kN at x=0 and 20 kN at x=4 m.
step_1.key_point: Define unknown reactions and load coordinates before equilibrium.

step_3.content: Take moments about A: \(\sum M_A=0\Rightarrow F_{R,B}\cdot4+10\cdot2=20\cdot2\).

step_5.content: Use vertical force equilibrium: \(\sum F_y=0\Rightarrow F_{R,A}+F_{R,B}-10-20=0\).

step_7.content: From the internal-force diagrams, read extrema: \(V_{\max}=15\,\text{kN}\), \(M_{\max}=20\,\text{kN*m}\).
step_7.key_point: Use diagram extrema for stress checks.
